topics.name: Adaptability
topics.description: Handling change, learning new tech stacks quickly

topics.name: Behavioral Core
topics.description: STAR method situational storytelling and self-awareness

topics.name: Teamwork
topics.description: Cross-functional collaboration, peer code reviews, supporting team goals

topics.name: Conflict Resolution
topics.description: Handling technical disagreements or communication friction constructively

topics.name: Leadership
topics.description: Taking ownership of tasks, guiding junior peers, driving initiatives

topics.name: Time Management
topics.description: Prioritizing tasks, meeting project deadlines, managing scope

topics.name: Failure & Learning
topics.description: Overcoming project setbacks, post-mortem reflections, personal growth

topics.name: Tell Me About Yourself
topics.description: Elevator pitch covering education, projects, skills, career aspiration

topics.name: Strengths & Weaknesses
topics.description: Self-awareness, key engineering strengths, areas of improvement

topics.name: Why This Company
topics.description: Company research, alignment with product mission and engineering culture

topics.name: Salary Expectations
topics.description: Professional discussion regarding entry-level compensation standards

topics.name: Career Goals
topics.description: Short-term and 3-5 year professional development vision

topics.name: Handling Rejection
topics.description: Resilience, seeking feedback, persistent skill development

questions.question: Tell me about a time when you had to learn a new technology or framework quickly for a project. How did you approach it?
hints: Use STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result)
Mention specific documentation and build steps
questions.expected_answer: S: Project required technology X. T: Master basics in 1 week. A: Read official docs, built demo mini-project, sought mentor code review. R: Delivered feature on schedule with 90%+ test coverage.
questions.explanation: Evaluates learning agility and self-directed problem solving.

questions.question: Describe a situation where you had a technical disagreement with a teammate. How did you resolve it?
hints: Focus on objective data and benchmark comparison
questions.expected_answer: S: Differing opinions on database design. T: Reach consensus without delaying sprint. A: Created quick benchmark POCs, discussed pros/cons objectively. R: Selected optimal hybrid approach, maintained positive relationship.
questions.explanation: Tests collaboration skills and emotional intelligence in team environments.

questions.question: Tell me about a project that failed or missed its deadline. What went wrong and what did you learn?
hints: Emphasize concrete steps taken to prevent reoccurrence
questions.expected_answer: S: Campus team project missed deployment deadline. T: Deliver core functionality. A: Underestimated integration complexity, conducted retrospective, added automated integration tests. R: Learned proper milestone breakdown and buffer estimation.

questions.question: Describe a scenario where you took the initiative to improve a process or codebase beyond your assigned tasks.
hints: Show positive impact on team workflow
questions.expected_answer: Noticed manual build steps were taking 20 mins. Set up local Docker scripts and automated test runner, saving team 15 mins per deployment.

questions.question: How do you prioritize your tasks when managing multiple deadlines during exam week or project sprints?
hints: Use prioritization framework (Eisenhower matrix, Urgent vs Important)
Communicate early if scope adjustment is needed
questions.expected_answer: Assess urgency and impact, break projects into daily milestones, focus on high-yield tasks first, and communicate proactively with stakeholders if delays arise.

questions.question: Tell me about yourself. Walk me through your academic background, projects, and key technical interests.
questions.expected_answer: Briefly state current degree/major, highlight 1-2 major technical projects or internships, summarize core skill stack, and end with passion for this specific software engineering role.
questions.explanation: Standard opening HR question to evaluate communication clarity.

questions.question: Why do you want to join our company as a fresher software engineer?
questions.short_description: Motivation for target company & role fit
hints: Reference specific products, tech blog posts, or company values
Connect your career growth to company mission
questions.expected_answer: Express genuine enthusiasm based on company's technology stack, product scale, impact, and strong mentorship culture for fresh graduates.
questions.explanation: Tests genuine interest and company research.

questions.question: What are your top technical strengths and what is one area you are working to improve?
hints: Strengths: backing with specific project achievements
Weakness: authentic area + active steps taken to improve
questions.expected_answer: Strength: Strong problem-solving and rapid debugging skills. Weakness: Tended to dive into coding too fast without detailed diagramming, now practicing upfront architecture sketching.

questions.question: Where do you see yourself professionally in the next 3 to 5 years?
hints: Focus on mastering software engineering craftsmanship, taking module ownership, and mentoring
questions.expected_answer: Aim to become a solid Mid/Senior Full-Stack Developer, taking complete ownership of key backend/frontend modules, writing clean resilient code, and mentoring junior engineers.
questions.explanation: Checks commitment to long-term professional technical growth.
